back_translation: Sarah's life was 127 years — the years of Sarah's life.
Sarah died in Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan, and Abraham came to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
Abraham rose up from beside the body of his dead wife and spoke to the Hittites, saying,
"I am a foreigner and a stranger among you. Give me a burial possession among you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight."
The Hittites answered Abraham, saying to him,
"Listen to us, my lord. You are a great prince favored by God among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will withhold his tomb from you for burying your dead."
Abraham rose up and bowed down to the people of the land, the Hittites.
He spoke to them, saying, "If you are willing for me to bury my dead out of my sight, listen to me and intercede for me with Ephron son of Zohar,
that he sell me the cave of Machpelah which is his, at the end of his field. Let him sell it to me at full price in your presence as a burial possession."
Now Ephron was sitting among the Hittites, and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the hearing of the Hittites — all who came to the gate of his city — saying,
"No, my lord, listen to me. The field I have given to you, and the cave that is in it, I have given to you. In the sight of my people I have given it to you. Bury your dead."
Abraham bowed down before the people of the land,
and he spoke to Ephron in the hearing of the people of the land, saying, "But if you will, please listen to me. I will give the price of the field. Take it from me, that I may bury my dead there."
Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him,
"My lord, listen to me. The land is worth four hundred shekels of silver — what is that between me and you? Bury your dead."
Abraham listened to Ephron and weighed out for Ephron the silver he had named in the hearing of the Hittites, four hundred shekels of silver, by the merchant's standard.
So Ephron's field, which is in Machpelah, which is before Mamre — the field, the cave that is in it, and all the trees within all its surrounding boundary —
was deeded to Abraham as a possession in the sight of the Hittites, before all who entered the gate of his city.
After this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah, before Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
And the field with its cave was deeded by the Hittites to Abraham as a burial possession.
